title: [sylveon] Battle Guide
summary: A bulky [fairy] special attacker whose [pixilate] [hyper-voice] spam is shielded by base 130 SpD and refueled through [wish].
strengths: Base 130 SpD plus 95 HP tanks one hit from nearly any unboosted special attacker
[wish] doubles as self-heal and team-heal utility
weaknesses: 2x Poison and 2x Steel — sludge-bomb and iron-head hit hard
Base 60 Speed loses the race against essentially every offensive pivot
Base 65 physical Def is easily cracked by any unresisted physical attacker

recommendedMoves.reason: Converted into boosted [fairy] STAB by [pixilate] — the main spam button
Punishes Steel switch-ins that would otherwise wall the set
Hits the physical Def stat to break special walls
Team and self recovery — converts defensive stats into sustain

recommendedItems.reason: Stacks passive recovery behind the base 130 SpD
Half-HP trigger for a one-time burst of survival

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[sylveon] is a pure [fairy] special attacker whose real identity in Champions MA is a bulky pivot. Base 130 SpD and 95 HP give it the special durability to sit in on most unboosted attackers, and hidden ability [pixilate] converts [hyper-voice] into boosted [fairy] STAB — producing a single attack that functions as both coverage and spam button. Base 110 SpA makes the damage real, not symbolic.

## Strengths
[pixilate] applies a 1.2x boost and a type change to [hyper-voice], so the main attack effectively hits in the 130 base-power neighborhood as a [fairy] move with no drawback. Base 130 SpD means most unboosted special attackers simply cannot break [sylveon] in a single turn, and 95 HP extends that into a multi-turn window. [wish] is the support glue — it heals [sylveon] on the swap turn while also delivering a full HP pool to an incoming partner, which is the single best use of [sylveon]'s defensive stat allocation.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Steel and Poison both hit for 2x, and any iron-head or sludge-bomb can take a huge chunk out of [sylveon] in one click. Base 60 Speed means [sylveon] essentially always moves last against offensive threats, so it can't trade cleanly. Physical Def 65 is the structural weakness — a mid-tier physical attacker with any unresisted move cracks through easily, and priority users like mach-punch can punish [sylveon]'s already-eroded HP. taunt also disables both [wish] and the defensive loop entirely, so trapping taunt users are a hard counter.

## Recommended Moveset
[hyper-voice] is the locked main because [pixilate] makes it the most efficient damage button [sylveon] has access to. [mystical-fire] handles the Steel matchup directly, which is otherwise a hard wall on the set. [psyshock] is the third slot because it hits the physical Def stat, which lets [sylveon] break special walls that would otherwise soak [hyper-voice] all day. [wish] is the fourth slot — it's what justifies [sylveon] over the other [fairy] attackers, turning 130 SpD into actual team-wide recovery. shadow-ball is the alternative to [psyshock] if the team prefers Ghost coverage.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default because it stacks turn-by-turn recovery behind a 130 SpD wall. [sitrus-berry] is the alternative for offensive teams that want a single-trigger burst of sustain against choice-specs nukes. The ability must be [pixilate] — cute-charm is a completely different, much weaker archetype. Tera [fairy] pushes [hyper-voice] into guaranteed-KO range against neutral targets, and Tera [water] flips the Steel weakness, turning iron-head into a manageable hit.

## Teammates & Synergy
[sylveon] pairs best with Ground or Fighting attackers that abuse the Steel and Poison pressure, because [sylveon] forces opponents to bring exactly those types into the fight. A stealth-rock lead multiplies the damage [sylveon] projects, and any partner that can absorb [wish] passes gains enormous longevity. Intimidate users also cover the physical-attacker weakness that [sylveon] structurally cannot handle alone.

## Play Patterns
[sylveon] is a long-game piece. Open with [wish] to lock in the recovery cycle, then spam [hyper-voice] on every neutral turn. Switch out against any live Steel, Poison, or physical threat; do not try to trade into those matchups. Tera [water] is the emergency button for the moment a sludge-bomb or iron-head is telegraphed — clicking it once is often the difference between losing [sylveon] and keeping it alive for a three-turn snowball. Never overstay against a taunt user.
